title: tilia leucocarpa var. glaucescens
intro: Tilia leucocarpa var. glaucescens, also known as the silver linden tree, is a deciduous tree species native to China. Its leaves, bark, and flowers have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating headaches and anxiety, and supporting cardiovascular health. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers are prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness their medicinal properties, and they can also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
